### DESCRIPCIÓN

In RSA d is a lot bigger than e, why don't we use d to encrypt instead of e?Connect with nc fickle-tempest.picoctf.net 49439.
### SOLUCIÓN

```
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Crypto3]
└─$ nc fickle-tempest.picoctf.net 49439
c: 
n: 
e: 

```

```

  GNU nano 8.6                            RSA                                      
c = 247338043895536375650388691361386003599700914148241331118895253845253729034170>
n = 849581124950644731095050664143965311770366576289039927072011694007402085489106>
e = 428750875484346610864667373634702667523583687751708295283270744275765088048990>

e = 65537
m = pow(c,e,n)
print(m)

flag = bytes.fromhex(hex(m)[2:]).decode()

print(flag)


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Crypto3]
└─$ python3 RSA
180638594769037903267909311328535969949661653394344568289572221
picoCTF{bad_1d3a5_3801255}

```
